_meta.dataSource: Synthesized from training knowledge (cutoff Jan 2026). WebSearch/WebFetch were permission-denied in this environment.
_meta.scope: Job platform UI trends 2024-2025 + anti-AI patterns

topTrends.name: Editorial typography & asymmetric grids
topTrends.description: Magazine-style layouts with intentional off-grid alignment, oversized serif/display headings (50-80px) paired with utilitarian sans body. Pioneered by Stripe Press, Linear changelog, Vercel /design.
topTrends.example: Linear's marketing site uses a 12-col grid but content blocks land at columns 2-7 or 5-12 — never centered. Reads like a printed page, not a SaaS landing.

topTrends.name: Data-forward job cards (no decorative bloat)
topTrends.description: Cards strip illustration and 3D mockups; surface match score, salary, recency, and skill tags as the primary visual hierarchy. Information density up, ornamentation down.
topTrends.example: Ashby and Wellfound (Y Combinator) list jobs as compact rows with salary band always visible — no 'Thỏa thuận' evasion, no hero photos.

topTrends.name: Tactile micro-interactions over scroll-jacking
topTrends.description: Replace heavy scroll animations with subtle hover physics: cursor-following spotlight, magnetic buttons, weight-based card lift. Framer Motion layoutId for shared element transitions between list ↔ detail views.
topTrends.example: Vercel dashboard hover-card glow that tracks cursor position via radial gradient mask.

topTrends.name: Monochrome + 1 accent (no gradient)
topTrends.description: Counter to the purple-blue gradient era — use a single saturated accent (often unexpected: lime, amber, electric blue) against pure neutrals. Gradients confined to background ambient lighting, never on buttons or text.
topTrends.example: Linear: pure black/white + electric blue accent only. No gradient buttons. Posthog: cream + hot pink accent.

topTrends.name: Semantic motion (not decorative)
topTrends.description: Animations explain state changes — skeleton morphs into content, list items stagger to draw eye to top result, status badges pulse only when fresh. Motion as feedback, not entertainment.
topTrends.example: Linear status changes animate the card border before the badge text, signaling 'something updated here' without a toast.

topTrends.name: Inline density with progressive disclosure
topTrends.description: Show 80% of information inline (no modal click-through for basics), expand for the remaining 20%. Job detail accordion inside the list row instead of a separate page.
topTrends.example: Notion-style inline expand of database rows. Ashby applicant pipeline shows status + last activity inline, expands to full timeline on click.

topTrends.name: Variable fonts for fine-grained hierarchy
topTrends.description: Use a single variable font (Inter, Geist, InterVariable) across weights 400-900 with fluid clamp() sizing. Avoids the bloat of loading 4 font files.
topTrends.example: Geist Sans from Vercel — single font covers UI labels at 12px through hero at 80px without secondary font.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Purple-to-blue diagonal gradient on buttons + text
avoidPatterns.why: This exact gradient (Stripe-inspired, popularized 2020-2022) is now THE marker of AI-generated UI. Every Lovable/v0/Bolt output uses it. Currently in JobHub globals.css — primary visual liability.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Glassmorphism cards (backdrop-blur + low-opacity bg + light border)
avoidPatterns.why: Peaked 2021, now ubiquitous in AI output. Reads as 'I asked ChatGPT to make it look modern'. Worse on dark themes where contrast suffers.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Hero with 3-column 'features' grid below + icon-circle each
avoidPatterns.why: Universal AI fallback layout. Every recruitment platform demo has 'Find Jobs / Apply Fast / Get Hired' as 3 icon cards. Reads as template.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Generic stock photography of diverse hands typing on laptops
avoidPatterns.why: Instantly screams 2018 stock site. Real platforms show product UI screenshots or no imagery at all.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Centered hero with massive heading + single CTA + 'trusted by' logo strip
avoidPatterns.why: Bootstrap landing page template — recognizable in 0.3 seconds. Use asymmetric layout or product-first hero instead.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Rainbow-colored skill tags with no semantic meaning
avoidPatterns.why: When every tag is a different color, color stops conveying information. Use single muted tag color + reserve color for status only.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Floating chatbot bubble bottom-right with sparkle emoji
avoidPatterns.why: AI-bolted-on signal. If AI is core, integrate it into the search bar or apply flow, not a corner bubble.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Excessive emoji in UI labels (✨ Apply Now, 🚀 Get Started)
avoidPatterns.why: ChatGPT default voice. Professional product UIs use icons (Lucide) not emoji.

avoidPatterns.pattern: Drop-shadow + 1px border + rounded-lg on every card
avoidPatterns.why: Tailwind default styling. Choose ONE separation method (border OR shadow OR background diff) per surface.

colorDirections.name: Editorial cream
colorDirections.mood: Warm, considered, almost printed. Cream background + near-black text + tomato-red accent. Differentiates from every other VN job site (all white) and from JobHub's current dark theme. Risk: less 'tech' feeling.

colorDirections.name: Industrial mono + lime
colorDirections.mood: Pure black/gray/white + electric lime accent (used sparingly for CTAs and active states only). Brutalist, confident, anti-gradient. Closest to Linear/Posthog aesthetic. Best fit for tech recruitment.

colorDirections.name: Deep slate + amber
colorDirections.mood: Reads like a developer tool (close to GitHub dark + a warm accent). Less aggressive than current purple, more grown-up. Salary numbers in amber pop without screaming.

colorDirections.name: Refined dark + jade
colorDirections.mood: Minimal departure from current JobHub palette — same dark base, swap purple-blue gradient for single jade-green accent (matches financial/'salary' semantic universally). Lowest-risk refresh path.

typographyTrends.rationale: One variable font covers 11px UI to 80px hero. Removes need for secondary display font and reduces bundle by 60%.
Magazine cover effect. Serif on hero + section titles signals 'craft', sans for tabular UI signals 'utility'. Strong differentiator from all 4 VN competitors (all-sans).
Salary ranges with proportional figures look uneven; tabular figures align columns visually. Small detail, huge polish boost on listing pages.
Single rule covers mobile → 4K. Removes 90% of `md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl xl:text-6xl` chain noise from Tailwind.
